edits.description: PR-mined: modeled after the real upstream commit 192c9356991df297a0c02f9d1c85a24e4022aacc 'feat(editor): no longer use enforced LTR base direction in editor' (Rosemoe, 2025-11-10), which moved TextBidi.getDirections off a hardcoded Bidi.DIRECTION_LEFT_TO_RIGHT onto Bidi.DIRECTION_DEFAULT_LEFT_TO_RIGHT. That commit is already baked into our pinned commit (TextBidi.java already uses DIRECTION_DEFAULT_LEFT_TO_RIGHT), so a literal forward-patch of its diff is a no-op. This edit continues the same real spirit ('stop enforcing a single base direction') with the natural next step: an overload that takes an explicit Bidi direction flag, with the existing single-arg method delegating to it.

assertions.name: behavioral_marker

edits.description: PR-mined: modeled after the real upstream commit 10370f833f496363d44a4d76380e29bd1e7e77e2 'perf(editor): avoid copying when create String in Latin1 implementation' (Rosemoe, 2026-03-03), which changed StringLatin1.newString() from a char[]-roundtrip to a direct new String(bytes, offset, length, ISO_8859_1) decode. That commit is already baked into our pinned commit's newString() (visible a few lines below this edit's change), so a literal forward-patch of its diff is a no-op. This edit applies the SAME real perf pattern to the sibling method appendTo(), which still had the pre-fix per-char loop.
